title: Configuring OpenClaw
description: Learn how to create the main configuration file, set model parameters, add a messaging channel, define an automation rule, and validate the result.

steps.instruction: Create the main configuration file `openclaw.json5` inside the mounted config volume.
steps.action: The terminal runs `docker exec openclaw openclaw init config` which scaffolds a default `openclaw.json5` with comments for every section.
steps.expectedOutput: The file is created at `/root/.openclaw/openclaw.json5`. The terminal echoes `Config file created at /root/.openclaw/openclaw.json5`.

steps.instruction: Set the default LLM model by editing the `model` block in the configuration.
steps.action: The configuration file is updated so that `model.provider` is `"openai"` and `model.defaultModel` is `"gpt-4o"`. The API key is sourced from the `OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variable.
steps.expectedOutput: After saving, running `openclaw config show model` prints `{"provider":"openai","defaultModel":"gpt-4o","apiKeySource":"env"}`.

steps.instruction: Add a Telegram channel under the `channels` array in the configuration.
steps.action: A new channel object is appended with `type: "telegram"`, `name: "my-bot"`, and `token: "$TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN"`. The config is written to disk.
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw config show channels` now lists one entry: `[{"type":"telegram","name":"my-bot","enabled":true}]`.

steps.instruction: Add an automation rule that greets new users who send their first message.
steps.action: An entry is added to the `automations` array: `{"trigger":"firstMessage","action":"reply","template":"Welcome to OpenClaw, {{user.name}}!"}`.
steps.expectedOutput: `openclaw config show automations` prints the new rule with trigger `firstMessage` and action `reply`.

steps.instruction: Validate the entire configuration to catch schema errors before restarting.
steps.action: The terminal runs `openclaw config validate` which parses the JSON5, resolves environment variables, and checks every field against the schema.
steps.expectedOutput: Validation passes with `Config OK - 0 errors, 0 warnings`. A summary lists the active sections: model, channels (1), automations (1).

completionMessage: Congratulations! Your `openclaw.json5` is valid and contains a model definition, a Telegram channel, and a first-message automation. Restart the Gateway to activate the changes.
